---
title: |
  </div>
  <div style="text-align:center">
    <img src="logo_b.png" width="350" /><br>
    Milestone Week-8<br>
    <span style="font-size:1em;">Mark Kirkpatrick DVM, MS</span>
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
    theme: cerulean
    tabsets: true
    df_print: paged
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    number-sections: false
    code-fold: true
execute:
  message: false
  warning: false
  echo: false
embed-resources: true
params:
  herd: "2108e"   # default value
editor: 
  markdown: 
    wrap: 72
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

if (!require("pacman")) install.packages("pacman")
pacman::p_load(
  broom,
  tidyverse,
  dtplyr,
  gt,
  arrow,
  rmarkdown,
  lubridate,
  quarto, 
  arrow,
  survival,
  survminer,
  ggsurvfit,
  flextable,
  gtsummary,
  marginaleffects,
  scales,
  zoo
  )

library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(lubridate)
library(ggplot2)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(tidyr)


# summary(animals)
# glimpse(animal_lactations)
# unique(events_formatted$event)
# unique(events_formatted_lt$status_lesion)
# write_csv(events_formatted_frsh,"excel_excuses.csv")
# str(events_formatted_frsh) 
# colnames(events_formatted_lt)
# colnames(animals)

```

# Report for `r params$herd`

## DATA Initial Loads

This is the section that performs the initial load of the Master data

```{r}
#| label: data_retrieve
#| include: true
#| echo: TRUE



# pull data 
# ==========================================================
events_formatted <- read_parquet(
  '../data/intermediate_files/events_formatted.parquet') 

animals <- read_parquet(
  '../data/intermediate_files/animals.parquet')

deno_cal <- read_parquet(
  '../data/intermediate_files/denominator_by_calendar_time_period.parquet') |>
  filter(deno_type == "lact_basic" &
           calendar_time_period_type == "year") |>
  select(-c(contains("dop"), day_of_phase_group))

# Create Herd Column and extract the herd based on position
events_formatted <- events_formatted %>%
  mutate(herd = str_sub(id_animal_lact, 18, 22))

# load GC's functions
source('../functions/fxn_load_os_fxns.R')
fxn_load_os_fxns()

# Load Parnell CR fucntions from Github
cr_url <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ParnellPharma/ParnellFunctionsPublic/main/parnell_calculate_CR_master.R"
source(cr_url)

# create variables for inline code
farm_name <- unique(events_formatted$herd)

date_max_pull <- max(events_formatted$date_event, na.rm = TRUE)
date_min_pull <- date_max_pull - years(1)

  
# load functions

# set up key vectors 


```



```{r}
#| label: open-html-r
#| eval: false

# 1. Define the file path
file_paths <- c("images/farm_report_2024_herd_2108e.htm",
                "images/farm_report_2024_herd_2755f.htm",
                "images/farm_report_2025_herd_2108e.htm",
                "images/farm_report_2025_herd_2755f.htm"
                )

# 2. Open the browser
walk(file_paths, browseURL)

```

## DATA Lame and Trim Organization

LAME and TRIM events were isolated and coded in preparation of potential use.

```{r}
#| label: data_organization
#| include: TRUE
#| echo: TRUE


# Filter 'events_formatted' for only LAME and TRIM to create 'events_formatted_lt'
events_formatted_lt <- events_formatted[events_formatted$event %in% c("LAME", "TRIM"), ]

# Code the lesions and diseases
all_lesions <- c("dd", "footrot", 'wld', "sole_ulcer","injury", 
                             "cork", "other", "hemorrhage",
                             "sole_fracture", "toe_ulcer", "thin",
                             "inf", "noninf", "toe", "axial", "lesion")

diseases <- c("inf", "noninf", "lesion")

# Apply the function to events_formatted_lt
events_formatted_lt <- fxn_code_lesions(
  events_formatted_lt,
  event_var = event,
  protocol_var = protocols_letters1,
  remark_var = remark_letters1
)

# Convert abscess designations to a "1" in the column noninf
events_formatted_lt$noninf <- ifelse(grepl("^Ab", events_formatted_lt$protocols_letters1), 1, 0)
# Convert abscess designations to a "0" in the column other
events_formatted_lt$other  <- ifelse(grepl("^Ab", events_formatted_lt$protocols_letters1), 0, 1)

# Apply the three classifying FUNCTIONS
events_formatted_lt <-
  events_formatted_lt |>
  fxn_collapse_lesions(lesions = all_lesions) |>
  fxn_trim_vars(
    location_event,
    id_animal,
    date_var = date_event,
    trimonly_var = trimonly
  ) |>
  fxn_dz_status(
    disease_cols = diseases,
    event_filter = trimonly == 0,
    gap = 1
  )

# Join the 'animals' to 'events_formatted_lt' and add listed variables
events_formatted_lt <- events_formatted_lt %>%
  left_join(
    animals %>% select(id_animal, date_left, date_sold, date_died),
    by = "id_animal"
  )

events_formatted_lt <- events_formatted_lt %>%
  arrange(id_animal_lact, date_event) %>%   # primary sort
  group_by(id_animal_lact) %>%
  mutate(
    # Sequence number within cow (by date)
    seq_date = row_number(),

    # Second sort sequence: within cow, order by event + date_event
    seq_event = row_number(order(order(event), date_event)),

    # Last event for this cow?
    is_last = seq_date == max(seq_date),

    # Was the cow removed at the last event?
    culled_last_trim = if_else(is_last & !is.na(date_left), 1, 0)
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    # Global censor date
    date_censor = max(date_event, na.rm = TRUE),

    # Days to cull
    days_to_cull = as.numeric(date_left - date_event),

    # Days to censor if no cull
    days_censor_cull = if_else(
      is.na(days_to_cull),
      as.numeric(date_censor - date_event),
      days_to_cull
    ),

    # Ever culled?
    culled_ever = if_else(is.na(days_to_cull), 0, 1),

    # 30‑day outcomes
    culled_30 = if_else(!is.na(days_to_cull) & days_to_cull <= 30, 1, 0),
    sold_30  = if_else(days_to_cull <= 30 & !is.na(date_sold), 1, 0),
    died_30  = if_else(days_to_cull <= 30 & !is.na(date_died), 1, 0)
  ) %>%
  select(-is_last)


# Add cohort and classification columns
events_formatted_lt <- events_formatted_lt %>%
  mutate(
    # DIM category bins
    dim_cat = case_when(
      dim_event >= 270 ~ ">270",
      dim_event >= 211 & dim_event <= 270 ~ "211-270",
      dim_event >= 151 & dim_event <= 210 ~ "151-210",
      dim_event >= 91  & dim_event <= 150 ~ "91-150",
      dim_event >= 30  & dim_event <= 90  ~ "30-90",
      dim_event >= 0   & dim_event <= 29  ~ "<30",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),

    # Floor dates to month
    floordate_fresh = floor_date(date_fresh, "month"),
    floordate_event = floor_date(date_event, "month")
  )


```

## DATA FRESH Events

A dedicated FRESH event data frame was created with the intent of describing the herd and understanding early removal patterns in the 1st 30 days of lactation.

```{r}
#| label: Create FRESH data
#| include: TRUE
#| echo: TRUE

# Create new FRESH data frame and filter for FRESH events
events_formatted_frsh <- events_formatted %>%
  filter(event == "FRESH") %>%
  
  # Join removal dates
  left_join(
    animals %>% select(id_animal, date_left, date_sold, date_died),
    by = "id_animal"
  ) %>%
  
  # Add censor, removal info, and quarter fields
  mutate(
    # Global censor date (computed once)
    date_censor = max(date_event, na.rm = TRUE),

    # Days to cull
    days_to_cull = as.numeric(date_left - date_event),
    
    # Days in milk from censor date
    days_in_milk = as.numeric(date_censor - date_event),

    # Days to censor if no cull
    days_censor_cull = if_else(
      is.na(days_to_cull),
      as.numeric(date_censor - date_event),
      days_to_cull
    ),

    # Ever culled?
    culled_ever = if_else(is.na(days_to_cull), 0, 1),

    # 30‑day outcomes
    culled_30 = if_else(!is.na(days_to_cull) & days_to_cull <= 30, 1, 0),
    sold_30  = if_else(!is.na(days_to_cull) & days_to_cull <= 30 & !is.na(date_sold), 1, 0),
    died_30  = if_else(!is.na(days_to_cull) & days_to_cull <= 30 & !is.na(date_died), 1, 0),
    
    # Pregnant?
    yn_preg = if_else(is.na(date_concieved), 0, 1),
    
    # Aborted?
    yn_abort = if_else(is.na(date_aborted), 0, 1),

    # Quarter fields based on date_fresh
    quarter_floor = floor_date(date_fresh, "quarter"),
    quarter_label = paste0(year(quarter_floor), " Q", quarter(quarter_floor))
  )

# Drop is_last only if it exists
if ("is_last" %in% names(events_formatted_frsh)) {
  events_formatted_frsh <- events_formatted_frsh %>% select(-is_last)
}


```

::: {.panel-tabset}

## ACTIVE HERD Tables

There are the herd lactational group dynamics and proportions.  While averages aren't the best metric to determine change they are presented to indicate certain performance status.  This is the ACTIVE herd only.

```{r}
#| label: Demographic tables
#| include: TRUE
#| echo: TRUE

library(dplyr)
library(scales)

# Filter to active animals
df_active <- events_formatted_frsh %>%
  filter(status == "active") %>%
  filter(herd == params$herd)

# Summarize by lact_group_5
tbl <- df_active %>%
  group_by(lact_group_5) %>%
  summarise(
    count = n(),
    prop = n() / nrow(df_active),
    avg_dim = mean(days_in_milk, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_preg = mean(yn_preg, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_abort = mean(yn_abort, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# Add total row
tbl_total <- tbl %>%
  summarise(
    lact_group_5 = "Total",
    count = sum(count),
    prop = 1,
    avg_dim = mean(avg_dim),
    avg_preg = mean(avg_preg),
    avg_abort = mean(avg_abort)
  )

tbl_final <- bind_rows(tbl, tbl_total)

# Format columns
tbl_final <- tbl_final %>%
  mutate(
    prop = percent(prop, accuracy = 1),
    avg_dim = round(avg_dim, 0),
    avg_preg = percent(avg_preg, accuracy = 1),
    avg_abort = percent(avg_abort, accuracy = 1)
  )

# # Print with title
# cat("\nActive Herd Demographics\n")
# print(tbl_final)

# Formatting with gt
gt_tbl <- tbl_final %>%
  gt() %>%
  # Title with herd parameter
  tab_header(
    title = md(paste0("Active Herd Demographics — **", params$herd, "**"))
  ) %>%
  # Rename columns
  cols_label(
    lact_group_5 = "Lactation Groups",
    count        = "count",
    prop         = "Pct of Herd",
    avg_dim      = "Avg DIM",
    avg_preg     = "Pct Preg",
    avg_abort    = "Pct Abort"
  ) %>%
  fmt_number(
    columns = c(count, avg_dim),
    decimals = 0
  ) %>%
  # Specify style of the table
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_body(
      rows = lact_group_5 == "Total"
    )
  )

gt_tbl


```

## Violin DIM Distribution

Herd averages are somewhat meaningless without some estimation of distribution.  A violin graphic was employed to examine differences.  A "Jitter" scatter plot was attempted but actually clouded the interpretation.

```{r}
#| label: Violin dim distribution
#| include: TRUE
#| echo: TRUE

# Filter data
df_active <- events_formatted_frsh %>%
  filter(status == "active") %>%
  filter(herd == params$herd)

# Build violin plot with axis control
ggplot(df_active, aes(x = lact_group_5, y = days_in_milk, fill = lact_group_5)) +
  geom_violin(trim = FALSE, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.15, outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "RdYlBu") +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 500),      # <-- set upper/lower limits here
    breaks = seq(0, 500, 50) # <-- set axis divisions here
  ) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = paste0("Days in Milk Distribution by Lactation Group — ", params$herd),
    x = "Lactation Group",
    y = "Days in Milk"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )



```

## FRESH Cohort Removal

The 1st 21-30 days of lactation is a crucial period for dairy cattle.  This section monitors potential "lethality" risk by monitoring the DIED proportion within 30 days of FRESH.  Overall removal risk (sold and died) is monitored by the proportion culled.

```{r}
#| label: Fresh Removal
#| include: TRUE
#| echo: TRUE


# =========================================================================
# 1. Filter and prepare data
# =========================================================================
plot_data <- events_formatted_frsh %>%
  filter(herd == params$herd) %>%
  filter(date_fresh > as.Date("2023-12-31")) %>%
  arrange(quarter_floor)   # ensures calendar order for x-axis


# =========================================================================
# 2. Summarize: % culled within 30 days + count
# =========================================================================
heat_data <- plot_data %>%
  group_by(quarter_label, lact_group_5) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    pct_culled_30 = mean(culled_30, na.rm = TRUE) * 100
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    pct_culled_30 = ifelse(quarter_label == "2025 Q4", NA, pct_culled_30)
  )

# =========================================================================
# 3. Build heat map
# =========================================================================
ggplot(
  heat_data,
  aes(
    x = quarter_label,
    y = lact_group_5,
    fill = pct_culled_30
  )
) +
  geom_tile(color = "black", alpha = 0.8) +

  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(
      round(pct_culled_30, 1), "%\n(n=", n, ")"
    )),
    color = "black",
    size = 3
  ) +

  scale_fill_distiller(
    palette = "YlOrRd",
    direction = 1,
    name = "% Culled Within 30 Days of Fresh",
    na.value = "white"
  ) +
  
  labs(
  title = "% Culled within 30 Days Fresh",
  x = "Quarter",
  y = "Lactation Group",
  caption = paste0("Fresh Cohort, ", params$herd)
) +

theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
theme(
  plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
  axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1),
  axis.title.x = element_text(size = 12),   # adjust x‑axis label size
  axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12),    # adjust y‑axis label size

  # Legend formatting for horizontal bar
  legend.position = "bottom",
  legend.key.width = unit(2, "cm"),
  legend.key.height = unit(0.3, "cm"),
  legend.title = element_text(size = 9),
  legend.text = element_text(size = 8)
) +
guides(
  fill = guide_colorbar(
    direction = "horizontal",
    title.position = "top"
  )
)


# =========================================================================
# 4. Summarize: % died within 30 days + count
# =========================================================================
heat_data <- plot_data %>%
  group_by(quarter_label, lact_group_5) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    pct_died_30 = mean(died_30, na.rm = TRUE) * 100
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    pct_died_30 = ifelse(quarter_label == "2025 Q4", NA, pct_died_30)
  )

# =========================================================================
# 5. Build heat map
# =========================================================================
ggplot(
  heat_data,
  aes(
    x = quarter_label,
    y = lact_group_5,
    fill = pct_died_30
  )
) +
  geom_tile(color = "black", alpha = 0.8) +

  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(
      round(pct_died_30, 1), "%\n(n=", n, ")"
    )),
    color = "black",
    size = 3
  ) +

  scale_fill_distiller(
    palette = "YlOrRd",
    direction = 1,
    name = "% Died Within 30 Days of Fresh",
    na.value = "white"
  ) +
  
  labs(
  title = "% Died within 30 Days Fresh",
  x = "Quarter",
  y = "Lactation Group",
  caption = paste0("Fresh Cohort, ", params$herd)
  ) +
  
theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
theme(
  plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
  axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1),
  axis.title.x = element_text(size = 12),   # adjust x‑axis label size
  axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12),    # adjust y‑axis label size

  # Legend formatting for horizontal bar
  legend.position = "bottom",
  legend.key.width = unit(2, "cm"),
  legend.key.height = unit(0.3, "cm"),
  legend.title = element_text(size = 9),
  legend.text = element_text(size = 8)
) +
guides(
  fill = guide_colorbar(
    direction = "horizontal",
    title.position = "top"
  )
)


```
:::

::: {.panel-tabset}

## HOOF Lesions Stacked

```{r}
#| label: Hoof Stacked Lesions
#| include: TRUE
#| echo: TRUE


# All lesion columns
lesion_cols <- c(
  "trimonly", "dd", "footrot", "wld", "sole_ulcer", "injury", "cork",
  "other", "hemorrhage", "sole_fracture", "toe_ulcer", "thin",
  "inf", "noninf", "toe", "axial", "lesion"
)

# Filter the dataset
df_filtered <- events_formatted_lt %>%
  filter(lact_group != "Heifer") %>%
  filter(herd == params$herd) %>%
  filter(date_event > as.Date("2024-07-01")) %>%
  filter(trimonly == 0)

# Pivot longer (keep ALL lesion types)
df_long <- df_filtered %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(lesion_cols),
    names_to = "lesion_type",
    values_to = "present"
  ) %>%
  filter(present == 1)   # keep only lesions that occurred

# Plot, but EXCLUDE lesion, inf, noninf
df_long %>%
  filter(!lesion_type %in% c("lesion", "inf", "noninf")) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = floordate_event, fill = lesion_type)) +
  geom_bar(color = "black") +
  labs(
    x = "Month/Year",
    y = "Lesion Count",
    title = paste0("Lesion Types by Month/Year — ", params$herd),
    caption = "Counts of All Non-Trim Lesions Over Time"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, NA),
    breaks = seq(0, 600, by = 50),
    labels = seq(0, 600, by = 50)
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  ) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "YlGnBu")



```

## HOOF INF NONINF

```{r}
#| label: INF v NONINF
#| include: TRUE
#| echo: TRUE


# 1. Summarize counts for "inf" and "noninf" from df_long
lesion_summary <- df_long %>%
  filter(lesion_type %in% c("inf", "noninf")) %>%
  group_by(lact_group_5, lesion_type) %>%
  summarise(total = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(lact_group_5) %>%
  mutate(percent = total / sum(total) * 100)

# 2. Recode lesion labels
lesion_summary <- lesion_summary %>%
  mutate(lesion_type = recode(lesion_type,
                              "inf" = "Infectious",
                              "noninf" = "Non-Infectious"))

# 3. Recode lact_group_5 from "LACT X" to "LGRP X"
lesion_summary <- lesion_summary %>%
  mutate(
    lact_group_5 = recode(
      lact_group_5,
      "LACT 1" = "LGRP 1",
      "LACT 2" = "LGRP 2",
      "LACT 3" = "LGRP 3",
      "LACT 4" = "LGRP 4",
      "LACT 5+" = "LGRP 5+"
    )
  )

# 4. Order lactation groups
lact_order <- c("LGRP 1", "LGRP 2", "LGRP 3", "LGRP 4", "LGRP 5+")
lesion_summary <- lesion_summary %>%
  mutate(lact_group_5 = factor(lact_group_5, levels = lact_order))

# 5. Horizontal 100% stacked bar chart
ggplot(lesion_summary, aes(x = lact_group_5, y = percent, fill = lesion_type)) +
  geom_col(color = "black") +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Infectious" = "red",
                               "Non-Infectious" = "grey40")) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(percent, 1), "%")),
            position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
            color = "white",
            size = 4) +
  labs(
    title = paste0("Infectious vs Non-Infectious by Lactation Group — ", params$herd),
    caption = "Filtered for non-trim lesions only",
    x = "Lactation Group",
    y = "Percent",
    fill = "Lesion Type"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
  )



```


## FOOT Lesion Removal Heat Mapping

```{r}
#| label: Removal_heat_map
#| include: TRUE
#| echo: TRUE

# Recode the status_lesion variable of 'NA'
events_formatted_lt <- events_formatted_lt %>%
  mutate(status_lesion = case_when(
    status_lesion %in% c("Chronic", "New", "Repeat") ~ status_lesion,
    TRUE ~ "Ctrl"
  ))

# Filter and prepare data with lesion relabel
plot_data <- events_formatted_lt %>%
  filter(lact_group_5 != "Heifer") %>%
  # setting the herd to filter for the report
  filter(herd == params$herd) %>%
  filter(date_event >= (max(date_event, na.rm = TRUE) %m-% months(12))) %>%
  mutate(
    status_lesion_label = case_when(
      status_lesion == "Chronic" ~ "Chronic Lesion",
      status_lesion == "Ctrl"    ~ "No Lesion",
      status_lesion == "New"     ~ "New Lesion",
      status_lesion == "Repeat"  ~ "Repeat Lesion",
      TRUE ~ "Unknown"
    )
  )


# =========================================================================
# 2. Summarize: % culled within 30 days + count
# =========================================================================
heat_data <- plot_data %>%
  group_by(status_lesion_label, lact_group_5) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    pct_culled_30 = mean(culled_30, na.rm = TRUE) * 100
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    # Remove heat mapping for Repeat Lesion
    pct_culled_30 = ifelse(status_lesion_label == "Repeat Lesion", NA, pct_culled_30)
  )

# =========================================================================
# 3. Build heat map
# =========================================================================
ggplot(
  heat_data,
  aes(
    x = status_lesion_label,
    y = lact_group_5,
    fill = pct_culled_30
  )
) +
  geom_tile(color = "black", alpha = 0.8) +

  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(
      ifelse(is.na(pct_culled_30), "NA", round(pct_culled_30, 1)), 
      "%\n(n=", n, ")"
    )),
    color = "black",
    size = 4
  ) +

  scale_fill_distiller(
    palette = "YlOrRd",
    direction = 1,
    name = "% Culled Within 30 Days",
    na.value = "white"   # ensures Repeat Lesion tiles are blank
  ) +

  labs(
    title = "% Culled - 30 Days by Lesion and Lactation Group",
    x = "Lesion Status",
    y = "Lactation Group"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1)
  )

# =========================================================================
# 2. Summarize: % died within 30 days + count
# =========================================================================
heat_data <- plot_data %>%
  group_by(status_lesion_label, lact_group_5) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    pct_died_30 = mean(died_30, na.rm = TRUE) * 100
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    # Remove heat mapping for Repeat Lesion
    pct_died_30 = ifelse(status_lesion_label == "Repeat Lesion", NA, pct_died_30)
  )

# =========================================================================
# 3. Build heat map
# =========================================================================
ggplot(
  heat_data,
  aes(
    x = status_lesion_label,
    y = lact_group_5,
    fill = pct_died_30
  )
) +
  geom_tile(color = "black", alpha = 0.8) +

  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(
      ifelse(is.na(pct_died_30), "NA", round(pct_died_30, 1)), 
      "%\n(n=", n, ")"
    )),
    color = "black",
    size = 4
  ) +

  scale_fill_distiller(
    palette = "YlOrRd",
    direction = 1,
    name = "% Died Within 30 Days",
    na.value = "white"   # ensures Repeat Lesion tiles are blank
  ) +

  labs(
    title = "% Died - 30 Days by Lesion and Lactation Group",
    x = "Lesion Status",
    y = "Lactation Group",
    caption = "This is not the full archive risk"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1)
  )


```

:::
